questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A manufacturer uses raw materials to produce p products each day. Suppose that each delivery of a particular material is $d, whereas the storage of that material is x dollars per unit stored per day. (One unit is the amount required to produce one product). How much should be delivered every x days to minimize the average daily cost in the production cycle between deliveries?
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. A manufacturer uses raw materials to produce p products each day. Suppose that each delivery of a particular material is $d, whereas the storage of that material is x dollars per unit stored per day. (One unit is the amount required to produce one product). How much should be delivered every x days to minimize the average daily cost in the production cycle between deliveries?
questions.solution_html:
If he asks for a delivery every x days, then he must order (px) to have enough material for that delivery cycle. The average amount in storage is approximately one-half of the delivery amount, or . Thus, the cost of delivery and storage for each cycle is approximately
 Cost per cycle = delivery costs + storage costs
 Cost per cycle = d +  ∙ x
We compute the average daily cost c(x) by dividing the cost per cycle by the number of days x in the cycle.
 c(x) =  + 
We find the critical points by determining where the derivative is equal to zero.
 c'(x) = -  +  = 0
 x = ± 
Therefore, an absolute minimum occurs at  days.

The average amount in storage is approximately one-half of the delivery amount, or (px/2) . Thus, the cost of delivery and storage for each cycle is approximately Cost per cycle = delivery costs + storage costs Cost per cycle = d + (px/2) ∙ x We compute the average daily cost c(x) by dividing the cost per cycle by the number of days x in the cycle. c(x) = (d/x) + (px/2) We find the critical points by determining where the derivative is equal to zero. c'(x) = - (d/(x) with superscript (2)) + (p/2) = 0 x = ± square root of ((2d/p)) Therefore, an absolute minimum occurs at square root of ((2d/p)) days.

questions.prompt_html: If f'(x) = 0 throughout an interval [a, b], prove that f(x) is constant on the interval.
questions.prompt_text: If f'(x) = 0 throughout an interval [a, b], prove that f(x) is constant on the interval.
questions.solution_html:
If a < x ≤ b, the mean-value theorem assures us that f(x) - f(a) = f'(t)(x - a) for some t in (a, x).
Since f'(t) = 0, we must have f(x) = f(a). This is true for all x in [a, b].
